title: Deploy 5-agent news workflow to new group and add cycle filter dropdown in UI
objective: Run two parallel branches. Branch A: deploy the 5-role news workflow into a new community group named 'news test', generate the required 5 agents from the openclaw-33 instance family, and run the workflow for two cycles. Branch B: add a dropdown in the top-right area of the conversation module so the UI can filter and display messages by cycle/round.

references.description: Source-of-truth role model and cycle-based news workflow semantics.

references.path: G:\community agnts\community agents\当前测试群组协议_初稿.jsonc
references.description: Current community protocol draft and existing simple news workflow baseline.
Previously validated baseline proving the current community can run bootstrap + simple formal workflow.

target_group.if_slug_taken: use a deterministic variant such as news-test-20260410 and report the actual slug

agent_topology.hard_rule: All four non-manager roles must be implemented as subagents or derived agents from the openclaw-33 instance family. Do not source them from unrelated existing agents.

parallel_branches.title: Deploy and run 5-agent news workflow

required_work.description: Create or provision one manager (33 base) and four derived subagents for editor, worker-33, worker-xhs, and tester roles.
Create the new group named 'news test', join the 5 required agents, and confirm role mapping in the group protocol or deployment metadata.
Deploy the news workflow into the new group using the 5-role model from the DeepFlow newsflow spec as the source of truth for roles and cycle semantics.
Run bootstrap/startup if required by the community runtime, then complete cycle 1 of the news workflow.
Run cycle 2 of the same workflow in the same group. The second cycle must be a real second round, not a copied artifact or log replay.
Collect evidence that both cycles produced distinct cycle/round identities and distinct conversation/artifact traces.

acceptance: A new group named 'news test' (or reported deterministic slug variant) exists.
Exactly 5 agents are present for this workflow topology: 33 as manager plus four derived subagents for editor, worker-33, worker-xhs, and tester.
The deployed workflow is not silently downgraded to the old 3-role simple workflow.
Two real cycles complete or a precise first design/runtime blocker is surfaced.
Artifacts or messages can be attributed to cycle 1 vs cycle 2.
do_not: Do not silently reuse neko/xhs/other unrelated agents as substitutes.
Do not quietly downgrade this request to the previously validated 3-agent simple workflow.
Do not fake the second cycle by duplicating logs, artifacts, or messages from cycle 1.
Do not blur a 5-role design/runtime incompatibility into a generic 'workflow failed'; classify the first real blocker.

parallel_branches.title: Add cycle filter dropdown in conversation module
parallel_branches.primary_owner: frontend thread, with minimal backend/projection support only if required

ui_target.location: top-right area inside the red-box conversation panel, matching the blue-box position from the user screenshot

required_work.description: Add a dropdown control in the specified top-right position of the conversation module.
Provide at least 'all cycles', 'cycle 1', and 'cycle 2' options once two-cycle data exists.
Filter the displayed conversation content by selected cycle/round so the message list only shows items belonging to that cycle.
If the frontend cannot reliably identify cycle membership from current projection data, add the minimum backend/projection field needed. Do not redesign the whole API.
Validate the dropdown against the new 'news test' group after cycle 1 and cycle 2 exist.

acceptance: A dropdown is visible in the requested top-right position of the conversation module.
Selecting cycle 1 only shows cycle 1 conversation content.
Selecting cycle 2 only shows cycle 2 conversation content.
An all-cycles option can restore the full mixed conversation view.
The solution uses explicit cycle metadata or explicit projection mapping, not brittle text guessing.
do_not: Do not place the control in another module or hide it in a debug area.
Do not rely on raw JSON text inspection in the browser to infer cycle membership.
Do not redesign the whole dashboard or add unrelated UI work.

controller_requirements.parallel_execution_rule: The main controller must open these two branches in parallel. Do not serialize branch B behind branch A unless a hard shared blocker makes that unavoidable.
controller_requirements.coordination_rule: If branch B needs minimal backend/projection support for cycle metadata, treat that as a focused repair inside branch B instead of collapsing both branches into one serial task.
controller_requirements.evidence_rule: Each branch must leave its own evidence files and log trail, then the main controller must publish one final structured summary that includes both branch outcomes.

deliverables.description: Main evidence folder for both branches.

working_principles: Do the work in parallel branches, not as one giant serial checklist.
Use the DeepFlow newsflow spec for role semantics; use the community runtime as the deployment target.
Do not silently simplify away the user's 5-agent / 2-cycle requirement.
For the UI branch, prefer minimal explicit data support over heuristic guessing.
If current community runtime cannot support the required 5-role two-cycle deployment, stop at the first real blocker with evidence instead of pretending the deployment succeeded.
